title: OK State Senator: "What am I supposed to do? Challenge the throne?"
author: Russell Hunter
orientation: A short report from a 2020 meeting between an Oklahoma State Senator and an abolitionist over SB13, used to illustrate how authoritarian Senate leadership under Pro Tem Greg Treat and Majority Leader Kim David intimidates rank-and-file Republicans into not advancing the abolition of abortion. The piece frames inaction on SB13 as a leadership-power problem rather than a vote-count problem.
audiences: Oklahoma voter wondering why a Republican supermajority hasn't ended abortion
Republican state senator weighing whether to defy leadership
Pro-life Christian deciding whether to support immediate abolition over incrementalism
Grassroots abolitionist activist planning legislative pressure tactics
Political journalist covering intra-GOP power dynamics

questions.question: Why does Free the States refuse to name the senator who made the 'challenge the throne' remark?
questions.audience: Political journalist covering intra-GOP power dynamics
questions.answer: The author says the senator would face retaliation from Senate leadership if his identity were revealed, so they protect him. This is offered as evidence of an oppressive internal culture in the Oklahoma Senate, not as journalistic reticence.
questions.quote: For the sake of the Senator who would likely face punishment if Senate leaders knew what he said, we will not reveal his name.

questions.question: What was the actual exchange that gave the article its title?
questions.audience: Oklahoma voter wondering why a Republican supermajority hasn't ended abortion
questions.answer: An abolitionist met cordially with the senator about SB13, broke into tears begging him to act to abolish abortion, and the senator responded by asking what he could possibly do, comparing dissent against leadership to challenging a throne.
questions.quote: Effected by her emotion, the Senator asked, “What am I supposed to do? Challenge the throne?”

questions.question: According to Russell Hunter, why do large Republican majorities so rarely deliver on conservative priorities?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian deciding whether to support immediate abolition over incrementalism
questions.answer: He argues that moderate and even left-leaning Republicans climb into leadership positions and then use that consolidated power to suppress conservative bills and the legislators who file them, including bills to abolish abortion.
questions.quote: Moderate and even left-leaning Republicans rise to positions of leadership and consolidate their own power which they use to squash conservative bills and legislators.

questions.question: What concrete punishment did Senator Joseph Silk face for backing immediate abolition?
questions.audience: Republican state senator weighing whether to defy leadership
questions.answer: Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat censured Silk over his support for immediately abolishing abortion, and the year before the article Silk was stripped of his Vice Chair roles.
questions.quote: Senator Joseph Silk has been severely censured by Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat for his support of immediately abolishing abortion. Last year, Silk’s Vice Chair roles were stripped from him.

questions.question: How did Silk himself describe the current Oklahoma Senate leadership compared to others he has served under?
questions.audience: Political journalist covering intra-GOP power dynamics
questions.answer: In a letter to colleagues after the punishment, Silk drew on his experience in the Coast Guard, multiple jobs, business ownership, and three different Senate leadership teams to say this was the most authoritarian leadership he had ever seen.
questions.quote: I have served in the U.S. Coast Guard under many different commands, held many different occupations, run a business and served in the State Senate under three different leadership teams and have never seen a more authoritarian leadership than we currently have.

questions.question: Who specifically does the article identify as wielding 'royal' power over the Republican caucus?
questions.audience: Grassroots abolitionist activist planning legislative pressure tactics
questions.answer: Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat and Senate Majority Leader Kim David are named as the king-and-queen pair whose threat of punishment keeps other Republican senators in line.
questions.quote: This is the type of oppressive power Treat and Senate Majority Leader Kim David exert over the rest of the Republican Senators who feel as though they are under the authority of a king and queen and that they will be punished if they step out of line.

questions.question: What does the author say is the human cost of senators not standing up to leadership?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian deciding whether to support immediate abolition over incrementalism
questions.answer: Hunter frames the consequence in blood: as long as Republican senators lack the courage to confront Senate royalty and the pro-life establishment, abortion in Oklahoma will continue, potentially without end.
questions.quote: And if Republican Senators don’t muster the courage to stand up to Senate royalty and the pro-life establishment, the blood could continue to flow indefinitely.

questions.question: What action steps does the article ask readers to take after reading?
questions.audience: Grassroots abolitionist activist planning legislative pressure tactics
questions.answer: It asks readers to subscribe to email updates because social media reach has been throttled, to do in-person activism using the 'Are You An Abolitionist' pamphlet and the 'Abortion is Murder And Everybody Knows it' quadfold, and to become monthly financial supporters.
questions.quote: Big tech is cracking down on our ability to reach you! Our social media reach has been greatly decreased in recent months.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Earlier profile of the same Pro Tem identified here as the king of the throne, providing back-story for his pattern of suppressing abolition.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Names and indicts the leadership figures (including Treat) whose intra-caucus power this article describes as throne-like.

related.title: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Documents the strategic plan the senators referenced here are quietly cooperating with, giving substance to the 'pro-life establishment' the article blames.

related.title: Watch - Of Giants & Heroes: Joseph Silk
related.reason: Companion piece on Silk, the senator whose punishment is offered here as the cautionary tale that keeps other senators silent.

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: Concrete example, published two days before this piece, of leadership-aligned committee gatekeeping against the same SB13 bill discussed in the meeting.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13"?
related.reason: Explains why SB13 specifically is the bill the abolitionist begged the senator to advance, clarifying the stakes of his refusal to 'challenge the throne.'

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Argues the opposition the senator fears is acting in bad faith, reinforcing this article's claim that capitulation to leadership equals capitulation to deception.
